presentation.name: Hi, I'm
presentation.title: Senior Software Engineer
presentation.subtitle: I build frontend systems that teams can keep moving in. My focus is the structural decisions that matter before they become expensive to change.
presentation.stack: React · Next.js · TypeScript · Node.js · Express · MongoDB · PostgreSQL

think.label: How I think
think.heading: The code is not the product. The solution is.
think.body: Messy code is not always a sign of bad decisions. Sometimes it was the right call at the time. I refactor when it starts slowing people down, not because the code bothers me aesthetically, but when the gain is real.

decide.label: How I decide
decide.heading: I default to simple
decide.body: Complexity is a cost. I add it only when there is a clear reason for it, and I make sure that reason is documented. Most of the time, simple is enough.

ship.label: How I ship
ship.heading: I own what I put out
ship.body: The code has to be easy to read, understand, and change without having to find me first.

collaborate.label: How I collaborate
collaborate.heading: I want the people around me to do well
collaborate.body: I get more satisfaction from unblocking someone than from shipping something alone. A team that moves well is worth more than any individual output.

hero.label: Philosophy
hero.heading: How I think about building software
hero.body: These are the principles I keep coming back to. Not rules I follow blindly, but patterns that have held up across projects at very different stages.

from-scratch.label: Starting from scratch
from-scratch.heading: Constraints are the design
from-scratch.body: When there's a blank slate, the hardest part isn't building. It's deciding what not to build yet. I start by defining the edges: what this system must never become. That clarity shapes every early decision, from data models to folder structure, and prevents the slow drift that makes greenfield projects feel like legacy code two years in.

legacy.label: Working with legacy code
legacy.heading: Read before you rewrite
legacy.body: Old code carries decisions made under pressures I wasn't there for. Before touching anything, I find what's load-bearing — the pieces other things quietly depend on. I move incrementally, one safe step at a time, so the team builds confidence in the codebase without betting everything on a full rewrite.

terminal.description: Interactive terminal to explore my portfolio and get in touch.
terminal.tagline: Senior Software Engineer · React · Next.js · TypeScript
